summary: A minimal implementation of a scalar-valued automatic differentiation engine and a small neural network library. It demonstrates the fundamental mechanics of backpropagation and the hierarchical structure of deep learning models.

concepts.name: Scalar Autograd Engine
concepts.subtitle: The core unit for tracking data and gradients
concepts.description: The Value class stores a scalar value and its gradient, acting as a node in a computation graph. It allows the system to track every operation performed on data to automatically compute derivatives later.
concepts.ask: How does the Value class keep track of the operations that created it?

concepts.name: Computation Graph Construction
concepts.subtitle: Building the DAG of operations
concepts.description: Every arithmetic operation (add, mul, pow) creates a new Value object that stores references to its parents. This implicitly builds a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that records the history of the forward pass.
concepts.ask: Why are the _backward functions defined inside the arithmetic methods?

concepts.name: Backpropagation Algorithm
concepts.subtitle: Computing gradients via the chain rule
concepts.description: Uses a topological sort to process the graph in reverse order. It triggers the _backward closures of each node, propagating the gradient from the output back to the inputs using the chain rule.
concepts.ask: Why is a topological sort necessary before calling the backward functions?

concepts.name: Neural Network Module
concepts.subtitle: Base class for parameter management
concepts.description: Provides a common interface for all network components to track their parameters and reset gradients. This abstraction allows complex models to be treated as a single unit for optimization.
concepts.ask: What is the purpose of the zero_grad method in the training loop?

concepts.name: The Neuron
concepts.subtitle: Basic unit of linear transformation and activation
concepts.description: Implements the standard linear combination of weights and inputs plus a bias, followed by an optional ReLU activation. It encapsulates weights and bias as Value objects, making them differentiable.
concepts.ask: How does the Neuron class integrate with the autograd engine?

concepts.name: Layer Abstraction
concepts.subtitle: A collection of parallel neurons
concepts.description: Groups multiple Neuron instances together to process an input vector into an output vector. It simplifies the network architecture by treating a group of neurons as a single operational block.
concepts.ask: How does the Layer class handle the transition from a single input to multiple neuron outputs?

concepts.name: Multilayer Perceptron
concepts.subtitle: The full neural network architecture
concepts.description: Chains multiple Layer objects together to form a deep network. It manages the flow of data from the input layer through hidden layers to the final output, enabling the learning of complex non-linear functions.
concepts.ask: How does the MLP class ensure that only the final layer is linear while hidden layers use ReLU?
